sections.paragraph:
μηδέποτε (μηδέ, ποτέ) adv. (X.; Pla. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; Jos., Ant. 14, 142, Vi. 259; Just.) never w. ptc. (Diod S 20, 78, 1; Just., A I, 49, 5; Galen, Inst. Log. 14, 7; PTebt 57, 6 [114 b.c.]) 2 Ti 3:7; MPol 2:3; Hm 2:3; 10, 1, 4; Hs 8, 7, 2. W. inf. 16:10.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

ptc. ptc. = participle

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Inst. Log. Inst. Log. = Institutio Logica

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

inf. inf. = infinitive

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 642
